questions.q: What was Vyasa's birth name?
questions.explanation: He was born Krishna Dvaipayana, named for his dark complexion and the island of his birth.

questions.q: Who were Vyasa's parents?
questions.explanation: Vyasa was born to the sage Parashara and the young ferry-girl Satyavati before her royal marriage.

questions.q: Why was Vyasa given the name Dvaipayana?
options: He was born on an island in a river
questions.explanation: Dvaipayana means island-born, for he was delivered on a river island in the Yamuna.

questions.q: Which three princes did Vyasa father to save the dying Kuru line?
questions.explanation: Through niyoga with the widowed queens and a maid, Vyasa fathered Dhritarashtra, Pandu, and Vidura.

questions.q: What great feat did Vyasa accomplish with the Vedas?
options: He composed all four Vedas himself
He divided and arranged the Vedas into four collections
He recited the Vedas to Ganesha
He hid the Vedas from the asuras
questions.explanation: He classified the single mass of Vedic hymns into four Vedas, earning the title Veda Vyasa.

questions.q: What does the title "Vyasa" itself mean?
questions.explanation: Vyasa means the one who divides and arranges, a title earned by his ordering of the Vedas.

questions.q: Vyasa's mother Satyavati later became the queen of which king?
questions.explanation: Satyavati married King Shantanu of Hastinapura after Bhishma's great vow cleared the way.

questions.q: To whom did Vyasa grant divine sight so the great war could be narrated to the blind king?
questions.explanation: Vyasa gave Sanjaya celestial vision so he could describe the battle of Kurukshetra to Dhritarashtra.

questions.q: Why was Dhritarashtra, Vyasa's first son by the queens, born blind?
options: A sage's curse lay upon the womb
Ambika shut her eyes during her union with Vyasa
He was struck blind by lightning at birth
His mother fasted through the whole pregnancy
questions.explanation: Queen Ambika closed her eyes in fear before Vyasa, so her son Dhritarashtra was born blind.

questions.q: Why was Pandu born pale?
options: Ambalika turned pale with fright during her union with Vyasa
He was born in the winter snows
A naga's venom touched him at birth
questions.explanation: Queen Ambalika blanched at the sight of the grim sage, and her son Pandu was born pale.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Vidura, Vyasa's third son?
options: A serving maid sent in the queens' place
questions.explanation: A serving maid sent in place of the frightened queens received Vyasa calmly, and her son was the wise Vidura.

questions.q: At whose request did Vyasa father heirs upon the widows of Vichitravirya?
questions.explanation: His mother Satyavati summoned Vyasa to save the Kuru line when her son Vichitravirya died childless.

questions.q: What is the name of the custom by which Vyasa fathered heirs for his dead half-brother?
questions.explanation: Niyoga allowed a sage or kinsman to father children upon a childless widow to continue the line.

questions.q: What promise did the newborn Vyasa make to his mother before departing?
options: He would return every twelve years
He would never leave her side
He would send his son in his place
He would appear whenever she thought of him
questions.explanation: Vyasa promised Satyavati he would come to her the moment she thought of him, a promise he kept.

questions.q: How did Vyasa pass his childhood after his miraculous birth?
options: He was fostered by the sage Kanva
He grew to manhood the moment he was born
He was carried to heaven for schooling
questions.explanation: Vyasa grew to full adulthood immediately upon birth and left at once for a life of austerities.

questions.q: What boon did Vyasa grant Gandhari that shaped the fate of the Kuru house?
options: A son destined to rule the world
questions.explanation: Pleased by her service, Vyasa granted Gandhari the boon of one hundred sons.

questions.q: What did Vyasa do with the hard ball of flesh that Gandhari delivered?
options: He buried it beneath a banyan tree
He cast it into the Ganga
He divided it into a hundred and one pieces nurtured in jars of ghee
He restored it whole to her womb
questions.explanation: Vyasa cut the mass into a hundred and one pieces and set them in jars of ghee, from which the Kauravas and Duhshala were born.

questions.q: While the Pandavas hid at Ekachakra, toward what event did Vyasa direct their steps?
options: The burning of the Khandava forest
questions.explanation: Vyasa visited the disguised Pandavas and guided them toward Panchala, where Draupadi's swayamvara awaited.

questions.q: How did Vyasa justify Draupadi's marriage to five husbands when Drupada objected?
options: A queen may wed all the brothers of a king
In a former life Draupadi had prayed to Shiva five times for a husband
Drupada had once promised her to five kings
The fire that bore her demanded five offerings
questions.explanation: Vyasa revealed that in a past life she had asked Shiva five times for a husband, so five were granted.

questions.q: What was the name of Vyasa's own sage son?
questions.explanation: Shuka, Vyasa's son, became a great sage and was among the first to learn the epic from his father.

questions.q: Vyasa is counted among the Chiranjivis; what does that mean?
options: One of the deathless who live on through the ages
questions.explanation: The Chiranjivis are immortals who endure through the age, and tradition counts Vyasa among them.

questions.q: After the war, what extraordinary vision did Vyasa grant the grieving Dhritarashtra and Gandhari?
options: A dream of Hastinapura restored to glory
The serpent sacrifice of a future king
The slain warriors rising from the Ganga for a single night
questions.explanation: By Vyasa's power the dead of Kurukshetra rose from the Ganga for one night to console the living.

questions.q: Which disciple of Vyasa recited the epic at Janamejaya's snake sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Vyasa had Vaishampayana recite the whole epic to King Janamejaya during the great sarpa satra.

questions.q: Whom did Satyavati first ask to father heirs on the widowed queens before revealing Vyasa's existence?
questions.explanation: Satyavati first begged Bhishma to wed the widows and sire heirs, and only after he refused did she summon Vyasa.

questions.q: Which three women did Vyasa lead away to forest retreat in their final years?
questions.explanation: Foreseeing the horrors to come, Vyasa took his mother Satyavati and the widowed queens Ambika and Ambalika to the forest.

questions.q: Vyasa is traditionally regarded as a partial incarnation of which deity?
questions.explanation: Tradition honors Vyasa as an amsha, or portion, of Vishnu in his form as Narayana.

questions.q: During the exile, Vyasa gave Yudhishthira the Pratismriti spell to be passed on to whom?
questions.explanation: The Pratismriti lore was meant for Arjuna, who used it to seek celestial weapons from the gods.

questions.q: Why did Queen Ambika shut her eyes when Vyasa came to her?
options: She was keeping a vow of darkness
Custom forbade a queen to look upon a sage
Vyasa's fierce ascetic appearance terrified her
Satyavati had commanded her to do so
questions.explanation: Vyasa's matted hair, blazing eyes, and grim ascetic form so frightened Ambika that she could not keep her eyes open.

questions.q: Vyasa's father Parashara descended from which great rishi?
questions.explanation: Parashara was the son of Shakti and grandson of the sage Vasishtha, placing Vyasa in Vasishtha's line.

questions.q: What gift did Vyasa offer Dhritarashtra before the war that the old king refused?
options: Divine sight to watch the war with his own eyes
A new son to replace Duryodhana
questions.explanation: Vyasa offered to give Dhritarashtra eyes to see the battle, but the king refused to watch his kin die, so Sanjaya received the sight instead.
